recipes.name: Dal Makhani
recipes.description: Rich and creamy black lentils simmered with kidney beans in a buttery tomato gravy, a quintessential Punjabi delicacy
instructions: Soak black lentils and kidney beans in water for 8 hours or overnight
Pressure cook soaked lentils and beans with salt and 4 cups water for 20 minutes until soft
In a pan, heat butter and sauté cumin seeds until they splutter
Add finely chopped onions and cook until golden brown
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant
Add pureed tomatoes, red chili powder and cook until oil separates from the gravy
Add the cooked lentils mixture to the gravy and simmer for 30 minutes on low heat
Stir in cream and garam masala, simmer for another 10 minutes
Garnish with a swirl of cream and serve hot with naan or rice

recipes.name: Paneer Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Grilled cottage cheese cubes in a spiced tomato-based gravy, a popular Punjabi restaurant-style dish
instructions: Cut paneer into 1-inch cubes and marinate with yogurt, half the ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and salt for 30 minutes
Make tomato puree from fresh tomatoes
Grill or pan-fry marinated paneer cubes until golden and slightly charred
In a pan, heat mustard oil and sauté cumin seeds
Add finely chopped onions and cook until translucent
Add remaining ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes
Add tomato puree and Kashmiri red chili powder, cook until oil separates
Add 1/2 cup water and simmer for 10 minutes
Add cream, garam masala, and kasuri methi, stir well
Add grilled paneer cubes and simmer for 5 minutes
Garnish with fresh cream and serve with rotis or rice

recipes.name: Chole Masala
recipes.description: Spiced chickpeas cooked in a tangy tomato and onion gravy with traditional Punjabi spices
instructions: Soak chickpeas in water for 8 hours or overnight
Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with the black tea bag and salt for 25 minutes until tender
Discard the tea bag and keep the cooked chickpeas aside
Make a paste from one onion and ginger-garlic paste
Make tomato puree from tomatoes
In a pan, heat mustard oil and temper with cumin seeds
Add the onion paste and cook until golden
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes
Add tomato puree and cook for 8 minutes until oil separates
Add chole masala powder, turmeric, and amchur powder, mix well
Add cooked chickpeas with their water and simmer for 20 minutes
Garnish with sliced onions and green chilies, serve hot with bhatura or puri

recipes.name: Aloo Gobi
recipes.description: Dry curry of potatoes and cauliflower cooked with traditional spices, a classic Punjabi home-style dish
instructions: Cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes and cauliflower into small florets
Blanch cauliflower in salted boiling water for 3 minutes, drain and set aside
Make a paste from tomatoes and ginger-garlic paste
In a wide pan, heat mustard oil and splutter cumin seeds
Add chopped onions and cook until light golden
Add tomato-ginger-garlic paste and all spices except garam masala
Cook for 5 minutes until oil separates
Add potato cubes and toss to coat with the masala
Cover and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally
Add cauliflower florets and mix gently
Cover and cook for another 10 minutes until potatoes are tender
Sprinkle garam masala and fresh coriander leaves
Serve hot with rotis or as a side dish

recipes.name: Punjabi Kadhi Pakora
recipes.description: Fried fritters in a tangy yogurt gravy tempered with spices, a traditional Punjabi comfort food
instructions: For pakoras: Mix 1 cup besan, baking soda, 1/2 tsp salt, and water to make a thick batter
Add finely chopped onion and potato to the batter
Heat oil and drop spoonfuls of batter, fry until golden and crispy
Drain on paper towels and set aside
For kadhi: Whisk yogurt with remaining besan, turmeric, and salt until smooth
Add 3 cups water and mix well
In a pan, heat 2 tbsp mustard oil and temper cumin and fenugreek seeds
Add ginger-garlic paste and red chili powder, cook for 1 minute
Add yogurt mixture and bring to a boil on medium heat, stirring continuously
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until kadhi thickens
Add prepared pakoras to the kadhi and simmer for 5 minutes
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves
Serve hot with rice or rotis

recipes.name: Gujarati Dal
recipes.description: A sweet and tangy lentil dish made with toor dal, jaggery, and tamarind, tempered with spices
instructions: Wash and soak toor dal for 30 minutes
Pressure cook dal with turmeric and salt for 3 whistles
In a pan, heat oil and add cumin and mustard seeds
Add asafoetida and let it splutter
Add tamarind paste, jaggery, red chili powder and cook for 5 minutes
Add the cooked dal and simmer for 15 minutes
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with rice or roti

recipes.name: Patra
recipes.description: Steamed colocasia leaves rolled with spiced gram flour batter, tempered with coconut and sesame
instructions: Wash colocasia leaves and dry completely
Mix gram flour with tamarind, jaggery, red chili, turmeric, garam masala and salt to make a thick batter
Apply the batter on each leaf and roll tightly
Steam the rolls for 20-25 minutes
Let cool and cut into thin rounds
For tempering, heat oil and add mustard, cumin seeds and sesame seeds
Add the cut patra pieces and toss gently
Garnish with grated coconut and serve

recipes.name: Methi Thepla
recipes.description: Traditional Gujarati flatbread made with fenugreek leaves, gram flour and wheat flour
instructions: Wash and finely chop methi leaves
Mix wheat flour, gram flour, methi leaves, turmeric, red chili powder, cumin seeds
Add yogurt, 2 tbsp oil and salt
Knead into a smooth soft dough using water
Cover and rest for 15 minutes
Make small lemon-sized balls
Roll each ball into thin circles
Cook on hot tawa applying oil on both sides
Cook until golden brown spots appear
Serve hot with pickle and yogurt

recipes.name: Khandvi
recipes.description: Soft and tangy gram flour rolls tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves and grated coconut
instructions: Mix gram flour, yogurt, turmeric, green chili paste, ginger paste and salt without lumps
Transfer to a heavy-bottomed pan and cook on medium heat
Stir continuously until the mixture becomes thick and leaves the sides of pan
Spread immediately on a greased plate to cool
Once cooled, cut into thin strips and roll carefully
Arrange rolls on a serving plate
For tempering, heat oil and add mustard seeds, curry leaves and asafoetida
Pour tempering over the rolls
Garnish with grated coconut and coriander leaves
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Undhiyu
recipes.description: Traditional Gujarati mixed vegetable casserole with sweet potatoes, brinjal, green beans and muthiya dumplings
instructions: For muthiya: Mix wheat flour, gram flour, fenugreek leaves, cumin seeds, turmeric and salt
Add water to make a thick batter
Shape into small dumplings and deep fry until golden
Cut all vegetables into large pieces
In a large pot, layer sweet potatoes and yam at the bottom
Add brinjal and green beans on top
Add fried muthiya and green peas
Mix spices with sugar and sprinkle over vegetables
Drizzle oil and add half cup water
Cover and cook on low heat for 30-40 minutes
Stir gently halfway through
Serve hot with roti or puri

recipes.name: Avial
recipes.description: A traditional Kerala mixed vegetable curry cooked in coconut oil and yogurt, featuring an array of vegetables cut into batons and finished with fresh coconut and curry leaves
instructions: Cut all vegetables into 2-inch batons of uniform size
Heat coconut oil in a large clay pot or heavy-bottomed vessel
Add cumin seeds and curry leaves, let them splutter
Add turmeric powder and green chilies
Add all vegetables and salt, toss to coat with oil
Add just enough water to cook vegetables partially
Cover and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes
Grind grated coconut with cumin seeds coarsely
Add the coconut mixture and yogurt to the vegetables
Mix gently without breaking the vegetable batons
Cover and cook until vegetables are tender but firm
Finish with a tempering of coconut oil, curry leaves and cumin

recipes.name: Vazhapoo Thoran
recipes.description: A delicate South Indian stir-fry made with banana blossoms, cooked with grated coconut, lentils and aromatic spices, traditionally served with rice
instructions: Remove the outer purple layers of banana flower until pale portions appear
Chop the inner florets finely and separate the tender center
Soak chopped flowers in water with lemon juice to prevent discoloration
Drain and rinse the flowers thoroughly
Heat coconut oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and urad dal
When mustard splutters, add broken red chilies and curry leaves
Add the banana flower, turmeric powder and salt
Sprinkle a little water and cook covered for 5 minutes
Add the chana dal and mix gently
Cover and cook until flowers are tender, about 8 minutes
Add grated coconut and toss until well combined
Cook uncovered for 2-3 minutes to evaporate excess moisture

recipes.name: Bisibelebath
recipes.description: A hearty one-pot Karnataka specialty combining rice, lentils and vegetables in a spiced masala, topped with fresh ghee tempering and served with boondi or raita
instructions: Wash rice and toor dal together and soak for 15 minutes
Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin, fenugreek, cloves and cinnamon
Add byadagi chilies and dried red chilies, roast until fragrant
Cool and grind with coconut and tamarind paste into a smooth paste
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add curry leaves
Add chopped vegetables and sauté for 3 minutes
Add the ground masala paste and cook for 5 minutes
Add soaked rice and dal, turmeric powder and salt
Add water (2.5 cups) and jaggery
Pressure cook for 4 whistles on medium heat
Let pressure release naturally and open the cooker
Mix gently from bottom to incorporate all ingredients
Prepare tadka with ghee, curry leaves and dried red chilies
Pour hot ghee tadka over the bisibelebath and serve

recipes.name: Gongura Pachadi
recipes.description: An Andhra specialty tangy and spicy chutney made with gongura (sorrel) leaves, served as a condiment with steamed rice and dal or as an accompaniment to rice dishes
instructions: Wash gongura leaves thoroughly and drain
Heat a heavy pan and dry roast urad dal and chana dal until golden
Add dried red chilies and roast for 30 seconds
Add gongura leaves and turmeric powder
Stir continuously until leaves wilt and become dry, about 5 minutes
Add tamarind paste and cook for 2 minutes
Remove from heat and let cool completely
Add coconut and garlic cloves to the cooled mixture
Grind everything into a coarse paste with minimal water
Heat coconut oil in the same pan
Add mustard seeds and let them splutter
Add the ground chutney paste and salt
Cook on medium heat, stirring frequently for 5 minutes
Remove from heat and serve with steamed rice

recipes.name: Keerai Kootu
recipes.description: A creamy Tamil Nadu lentil and spinach stew tempered with coconut, cumin and pepper, tempered with ghee and served with rice or roti
instructions: Wash spinach thoroughly and chop roughly
Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and water until soft and mushy
Grind coconut with cumin seeds and peppercorns into a smooth paste
Heat ghee in a vessel, add mustard seeds and urad dal
When mustard splutters, add broken red chilies and curry leaves
Add asafoetida and immediately add chopped spinach
Stir spinach until it wilts and reduces in volume, about 3 minutes
Add the cooked toor dal and salt, mix well
Add the coconut paste and bring to a gentle simmer
Cook for 5 minutes until flavors meld together
Adjust consistency with water if needed
Finish with a final tadka of ghee, mustard and curry leaves
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Shukto
recipes.description: A bitter-sweet mixed vegetable curry, a traditional Bengali starter.
instructions: Cut all vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
Heat ghee and mustard oil in a pan.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add bitter gourd and fry until slightly browned.
Add other vegetables, turmeric, and salt.
Add water and sugar, cover and simmer until cooked.
Serve hot as a starter.

recipes.name: Aloo Posto
recipes.description: Potatoes cooked with poppy seeds, a classic Bengali comfort food.
instructions: Soak poppy seeds in water for 15 minutes.
Peel and cut potatoes into cubes.
Grind poppy seeds to a paste.
Heat mustard oil, add cumin seeds and green chillies.
Add potatoes and fry until golden.
Add poppy seed paste, salt, and water.
Cover and cook until potatoes are tender.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.name: Labra
recipes.description: A mixed vegetable stew with potatoes, pumpkins, and legumes.
instructions: Cut all vegetables into pieces.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds.
Add ginger and fry for a minute.
Add vegetables, turmeric, and salt.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Simmer until vegetables are cooked.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

recipes.name: Chaler Dal
recipes.description: Simple lentil soup with rice, a staple Bengali dish.
instructions: Wash rice and dal.
Pressure cook rice and dal with water and turmeric.
In a small pan, heat ghee and add cumin seeds.
Pour the tempering over the dal and rice.
Add salt and mix well.
Serve hot.

recipes.name: Begun Bhaja
recipes.description: Crispy fried brinjals, a popular Bengali side dish.
instructions: Slice brinjals into thin rounds.
Apply turmeric and salt.
Heat mustard oil in a pan.
Fry brinjal slices until golden and crispy.
Serve hot as a side dish.

recipes.name: Bharli Vangi
recipes.description: Maharashtrian stuffed brinjals (eggplants) in a sweet, tangy and nutty sauce.
instructions: Wash brinjals, make a cross cut at the base, leaving the stems intact.
Mix besan, coconut, peanuts, jaggery, tamarind pulp, green chilies, cumin, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt in a bowl to make the stuffing.
Stuff each brinjal with the mixture, ensuring it fills the cuts.
Heat oil in a deep pan, add cumin seeds, sauté until fragrant, then add the stuffed brinjals.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until brinjals are tender and the sauce thickens.
Serve hot with chapati or rice.

recipes.name: Puran Poli
recipes.description: Sweet flatbread filled with cooked chana dal and jaggery, a classic Maharashtrian festive dish.
instructions: Pressure cook chana dal with water until soft, then mash well.
In a pan, combine mashed dal with jaggery and a pinch of turmeric, cook on medium heat until the mixture thickens and becomes a sweet filling, about 10 minutes.
Prepare the dough by mixing wheat flour, salt, and enough water to form a soft, pliable dough.
Divide the dough into equal portions, roll each into a small disc, place a spoonful of the dal-jaggery filling in the center, and seal.
Roll out each stuffed portion gently into a thin flatbread.
Cook each Poli on a hot tawa with ghee, turning until both sides are golden and cooked through.
Serve warm with a drizzle of ghee.

recipes.name: Dal Baati Churma
recipes.description: A traditional Rajasthani feast featuring spiced lentil curry served with crispy baked wheat balls (baati) and sweet churma crumble.
instructions: Soak toor dal and chana dal for 30 minutes, then pressure cook with turmeric and salt for 3 whistles.
Mix wheat flour, ghee, cumin seeds, asafoetida, red chili powder, and enough water to make a stiff dough.
Shape dough into small balls (baatis) and flatten slightly.
Bake baatis in a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) for 30 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and crisp.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and asafoetida, then add the cooked dal and simmer for 10 minutes.
Crumble baked baatis and mix with jaggery and sugar to make churma.
Serve dal in a bowl, top with hot baatis and sprinkle churma over the top.

recipes.name: Gatte ki Sabzi
recipes.description: Gram flour dumplings cooked in a spiced yogurt gravy, a classic Rajasthani dish with a rich, tangy flavor.
instructions: Mix gram flour with a pinch of turmeric, red chili powder, cumin, and salt; add water to form a thick dough.
Shape dough into small cylinders and steam or boil for 15 minutes until cooked; let cool and cut into pieces.
Heat ghee in a pan, add mustard and cumin seeds; let them splutter.
Add asafoetida, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and whisked yogurt; stir until smooth.
Add water, bring to a gentle boil, then add gatta pieces and simmer for 10 minutes until gravy thickens.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with roti or rice.

recipes.name: Rajasthani Kadhi
recipes.description: A light, tangy yogurt-based curry with gram flour fritters, flavored with fenugreek and curry leaves.
instructions: Whisk yogurt with gram flour, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt until smooth.
Add water and mix to make a thin batter.
Heat ghee in a deep pan, add cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves; sauté briefly.
Pour yogurt batter into the pan, stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until kadhi thickens.
Add sugar (optional), adjust salt, and serve hot garnished with fresh cilantro.

recipes.name: Chettinad Vegetable Kuzhambu
recipes.description: A tangy, spiced tamarind and vegetable gravy typical of Chettinad cuisine, packed with roasted spices and fresh curry leaves.

ingredients.name: Chettinad spice mix (coriander, cumin, pepper, red chilies, fenugreek)

instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat; add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves and onions; sauté until onions are golden.
Stir in garlic, ginger, and Chettinad spice mix; cook for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes, brinjal, okra, and green peas; mix well.
Dissolve tamarind paste in water, add to the pot, and add turmeric and salt.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Adjust seasoning, garnish with fresh curry leaves, and serve hot with rice or roti.

recipes.name: Ker Sangri
recipes.description: A traditional Rajasthani side dish made with dried ker beans and sangri berries, cooked with aromatic spices.
instructions: Soak ker beans and sangri berries in water overnight; drain and rinse.
Heat mustard oil in a pan; add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add the soaked ker and sangri to the pan and sauté for 5 minutes.
Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add water and bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer for 30-35 minutes until the beans are tender.
Add sugar and cook for another 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve as a side dish.

recipes.name: Kashmiri Dum Aloo
recipes.description: A classic Kashmiri potato curry cooked in a spiced yogurt gravy, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Boil the potatoes until just tender, peel and prick them with a fork.
Heat mustard oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, and let them splutter.
Add ginger, green chilies, and sauté for a minute.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala; stir.
Add beaten yogurt gradually, stirring continuously to avoid curdling.
Add the boiled potatoes and salt, simmer for 10-15 minutes until gravy thickens.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice or naan.

recipes.name: Chettinad Vegetable Kootu
recipes.description: A hearty spiced lentil and vegetable stew typical of the Chettinad region, made with toor dal, mixed vegetables, and a aromatic coconut oil tempering.
instructions: Rinse the toor dal and pressure cook with 3 cups water and turmeric for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat coconut oil in a large pan, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, dried red chillies, and asafoetida; sauté until fragrant.
Add the chopped mixed vegetables and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cooked toor dal and salt, mix well, and simmer for 10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with rice or roti.

recipes.name: Chettinad Tomato Curry
recipes.description: A tangy and spiced tomato-based curry that showcases the bold flavors of Chettinad cuisine, perfect as a side dish for rice or roti.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds; sauté until the mustard seeds splutter.
Add the dried red chillies and green chillies, then add the sliced onion and garlic; cook until onion is golden.
Add the chopped tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt; mix well.
Cover and cook on medium heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes are soft and oil separates.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

recipes.name: Fiš-paštić
recipes.description: A traditional Slavonian fish stew made with river fish, potatoes and vegetables in a savory broth
instructions: Clean and cut fish into portions, season with salt and pepper
Cut potatoes into cubes and carrots into slices
Sauté onions in oil until translucent, add garlic
Add tomatoes and cook until soft
Add potatoes, carrots and broth, simmer for 15 minutes
Add fish pieces and cook for 10-15 minutes until fish is done
Season to taste and serve with fresh bread

recipes.name: Čobanac
recipes.description: A hearty Slavonian goulash with beef, vegetables and paprika, slow-cooked to perfection
instructions: Cut beef into large cubes, season with salt and pepper
Sauté onions in oil until golden
Add beef and brown on all sides
Stir in paprika and cook for 2 minutes
Add tomatoes, potatoes and carrots
Add enough water to cover, add bay leaves
Simmer on low heat for 2-3 hours until meat is tender
Serve with fresh bread or polenta

recipes.name: Sataraš
recipes.description: A rustic Slavonian vegetable stew with peppers, tomatoes and onions, perfect for summer
instructions: Core and slice bell peppers into strips
Blanch and peel tomatoes, then chop roughly
Sauté onion in oil until soft
Add peppers and cook until they start to soften
Add tomatoes, garlic, sugar, salt and pepper
Simmer uncovered for 30-40 minutes until thick
Serve as a side dish or over pasta

recipes.name: Šukijada
recipes.description: A traditional Slavonian bean stew made with white beans and smoked pork
instructions: Soak beans overnight, drain and rinse
Brown smoked pork in a pot, remove and set aside
Sauté onions, carrots, celery and garlic in the pot
Add beans, tomatoes, bay leaves and enough water
Bring to boil, then simmer for 1 hour
Add smoked pork and cook for another 30-45 minutes
Season to taste and serve with bread

recipes.name: Brudet
recipes.description: A Dalmatian fish stew with white wine, tomatoes and Mediterranean herbs
instructions: Cut fish into portions, season with salt and pepper
Sauté onions in olive oil until translucent
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute
Add tomatoes and white wine, simmer for 10 minutes
Add fish pieces and enough water to cover
Add bay leaves and simmer for 15-20 minutes
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve

recipes.name: Pašticada
recipes.description: A Dalmatian specialty of beef marinated in wine and vinegar, then slow-cooked with prunes
instructions: Marinate beef in wine and vinegar overnight
Remove beef, pat dry, and season with salt and pepper
Brown beef in oil with bacon, remove and set aside
Sauté onions and carrots, add tomato paste
Return beef, add strained marinade, and bay leaves
Add prunes and enough water to cover
Braze in oven at 160°C for 2-3 hours until tender
Slice thinly and serve with cooking juices

recipes.name: Maneštra
recipes.description: A Dalmatian minestrone with beans, vegetables and small pasta
instructions: Soak beans overnight, cook until tender
Sauté onion, carrots, and celery in olive oil
Add potatoes, zucchini, and tomatoes
Add cooked beans and enough water
Simmer for 15 minutes, then add pasta
Cook until pasta is al dente
Season and garnish with parsley

recipes.name: Crni rižot
recipes.description: A Venetian-inspired Dalmatian black risotto made with cuttlefish ink
instructions: Clean and chop cuttlefish into small pieces
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil
Add cuttlefish and cook for 5 minutes
Add rice and toast for 2 minutes
Pour in white wine and let evaporate
Add broth gradually, stirring constantly
In the last 5 minutes, add cuttlefish ink
Season, garnish with parsley and serve

recipes.name: Štrukli
recipes.description: Traditional dumplings filled with cottage cheese, a beloved Croatian comfort food
instructions: Make dough with flour, eggs, salt and water, rest for 30 minutes
Mix cottage cheese with sugar, one egg and sour cream
Roll dough thin, spread filling evenly
Roll tightly and cut into pieces
Cook in salted boiling water for 10-15 minutes
Drain and top with buttered breadcrumbs
Serve warm with additional sour cream

recipes.name: Burek
recipes.description: Flaky phyllo pastry filled with cheese and eggs, a popular breakfast throughout Croatia
instructions: Mix cheese with eggs, sour cream, sugar and salt
Brush a baking pan with melted butter
Layer phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter
Spread cheese filling evenly
Roll up and place seam-side down in pan
Brush top with remaining butter
Bake at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden
Cut into squares and serve warm

recipes.name: Sarma
recipes.description: Cabbage leaves stuffed with minced meat and rice, slow-cooked in sauerkraut juice
instructions: Mix ground meats with rice, egg, onion, salt and paprika
Separate sauerkraut leaves, remove tough stems
Place filling on each leaf, roll and tuck ends
Layer bacon in pot, arrange rolls tightly
Add tomato paste and water to cover
Simmer on low heat for 2-3 hours
Serve with bread and sour cream

recipes.name: Pašteta
recipes.description: A smooth liver pate spread made with pork liver and onions, perfect on fresh bread
instructions: Clean liver, remove any sinews
Fry bacon until crispy, remove and set aside
Sauté onions until golden
Add liver and cook until no longer pink
Add garlic and marjoram, cook 2 more minutes
Blend everything with eggs and butter until smooth
Season with salt and pepper
Refrigerate until firm, serve on bread

recipes.name: Dinstana leća
recipes.description: Braised lentils with caramelized onions and spices, a warming continental dish
instructions: Sauté onions slowly until deep golden, about 20 minutes
Add garlic, cumin and cook 1 minute
Add lentils, broth, bay leaves and carrots
Bring to boil, then simmer for 30-40 minutes
Season with salt and pepper
Serve with crusty bread

recipes.name: Grašković
recipes.description: A creamy pea stew with potatoes and carrots, a traditional comfort food
instructions: Sauté onion in butter until translucent
Add carrots and potatoes, cook for 5 minutes
Add broth and simmer until vegetables are tender
Add peas and cook for 10 more minutes
Stir in sour cream and fresh dill
Season and serve with fresh bread

recipes.name: Buhtle
recipes.description: Fluffy fried doughnuts dusted with sugar, a beloved sweet treat from Dalmatia
instructions: Mix flour with yeast and sugar, add warm milk
Add eggs, butter and salt, knead until smooth
Let dough rise for 1 hour until doubled
Divide into balls, let rest 15 minutes
Fry in oil at 170°C until golden on all sides
Drain and dust generously with powdered sugar
Serve warm

recipes.name: Kremšnite
recipes.description: Creamy vanilla slice pastries with custard filling, Croatia's most famous dessert
instructions: Brush phyllo with butter, layer in baking pan
Bake at 180°C until golden and crisp
Heat milk with vanilla
Whisk yolks with sugar until pale
Add cornstarch and mix until smooth
Pour into milk, cook stirring until thick
Pour custard over phyllo base, chill 4 hours
Top with whipped cream and dust with powdered sugar

recipes.name: Štrudla od jabuka
recipes.description: Traditional apple strudel with thin crispy phyllo, cinnamon and raisins
instructions: Peel and slice apples thinly
Mix apples with sugar, cinnamon, raisins and lemon
Layer phyllo sheets brushing with butter
Sprinkle breadcrumbs over phyllo
Spread apple filling evenly
Roll tightly and place seam-side down
Brush with remaining butter
Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until golden
Dust with powdered sugar and serve warm

recipes.name: Fižol u pavlaci
recipes.description: Braised beans in sour cream sauce, a simple and satisfying traditional dish
instructions: Soak beans overnight, cook until tender
Sauté onion and garlic in butter
Add beans and broth, simmer for 15 minutes
Stir in sour cream and heat through
Season with salt and pepper
Garnish with parsley and serve

recipes.name: Lećenik
recipes.description: A savory lentil pie with eggs and herbs, traditional from Dalmatian inland
instructions: Cook lentils until soft, drain any excess water
Sauté onion and garlic in 1 tbsp oil
Mix lentils with sautéed onion, eggs, parsley, flour
Season with salt and pepper
Heat remaining oil in pan, add lentil mixture
Cook covered for 10 minutes, flip and cook other side
Serve warm with salad

recipes.name: Zelena salata
recipes.description: A fresh garden salad with olive oil and lemon dressing, perfect year-round
instructions: Wash and dry all greens thoroughly
Tear greens into bite-sized pieces
Slice cucumber and radishes thinly
Slice spring onions
Combine all vegetables in a bowl
Whisk olive oil with lemon juice, salt and pepper
Drizzle dressing over salad just before serving
Toss gently and serve immediately
